channel.title: Context Engineering Daily
channel.description: Daily news and research updates in AI Context Engineering, Prompt Engineering, RAG, and LLM development

item.title: Recursive Language Models Meet Uncertainty: The Surprising Effectiveness of Self-Reflective Program Search for Long Context
item.description: arXiv:2603.15653v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Long-context handling remains a core challenge for language models: even with extended context windows, models often fail to reliably extract, reason over, and use the information across long contexts. Recent works like Recursive Language Models (RLM)...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, model, reasoning, paper, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: MedArena: Comparing LLMs for Medicine-in-the-Wild Clinician Preferences
item.description: arXiv:2603.15677v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly central to clinician workflows, spanning clinical decision support, medical education, and patient communication. However, current evaluation methods for medical LLMs rely heavily on static, templated benc...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, template, large language model, GPT, platform | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: MiroThinker-1.7 & H1: Towards Heavy-Duty Research Agents via Verification
item.description: arXiv:2603.15726v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present MiroThinker-1.7, a new research agent designed for complex long-horizon reasoning tasks. Building on this foundation, we further introduce MiroThinker-H1, which extends the agent with heavy-duty reasoning capabilities for more reliable mult...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> release, model, reasoning, analysis, research | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Morphemes Without Borders: Evaluating Root-Pattern Morphology in Arabic Tokenizers and LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2603.15773v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This work investigates how effectively large language models (LLMs) and their tokenization schemes represent and generate Arabic root-pattern morphology, probing whether they capture genuine morphological structure or rely on surface memorization. Ara...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, analysis, large language model, study, alignment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: COGNAC at SemEval-2026 Task 5: LLM Ensembles for Human-Level Word Sense Plausibility Rating in Challenging Narratives
item.description: arXiv:2603.15897v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We describe our system for SemEval-2026 Task 5, which requires rating the plausibility of given word senses of homonyms in short stories on a 5-point Likert scale. Systems are evaluated by the unweighted average of accuracy (within one standard deviat...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> zero-shot, experiment, reasoning, model, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: BANGLASOCIALBENCH: A Benchmark for Evaluating Sociopragmatic and Cultural Alignment of LLMs in Bangladeshi Social Interaction
item.description: arXiv:2603.15949v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models have demonstrated strong multilingual fluency, yet fluency alone does not guarantee socially appropriate language use. In high-context languages, communicative competence requires sensitivity to social hierarchy, relational roles...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> zero-shot, model, reasoning, large language model, alignment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: POLAR:A Per-User Association Test in Embedding Space
item.description: arXiv:2603.15950v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Most intrinsic association probes operate at the word, sentence, or corpus level, obscuring author-level variation. We present POLAR (Per-user On-axis Lexical Association Re-port), a per-user lexical association test that runs in the embedding space o...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, embedding, alignment, vector, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Family of LLMs Liberated from Static Vocabularies
item.description: arXiv:2603.15953v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Tokenization is a central component of natural language processing in current large language models (LLMs), enabling models to convert raw text into processable units. Although learned tokenizers are widely adopted, they exhibit notable limitations, i...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> transformer, fine-tuning, model, embedding, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Neural-Symbolic Logic Query Answering in Non-Euclidean Space
item.description: arXiv:2603.15633v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Answering complex first-order logic (FOL) queries on knowledge graphs is essential for reasoning. Symbolic methods offer interpretability but struggle with incomplete graphs, while neural approaches generalize better but lack transparency. Neural-symb...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, model, reasoning, embedding, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: NextMem: Towards Latent Factual Memory for LLM-based Agents
item.description: arXiv:2603.15634v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Memory is critical for LLM-based agents to preserve past observations for future decision-making, where factual memory serves as its foundational part. However, existing approaches to constructing factual memory face several limitations. Textual metho...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> release, experiment, model, retrieval, memory | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: The Comprehension-Gated Agent Economy: A Robustness-First Architecture for AI Economic Agency
item.description: arXiv:2603.15639v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: AI agents are increasingly granted economic agency (executing trades, managing budgets, negotiating contracts, and spawning sub-agents), yet current frameworks gate this agency on capability benchmarks that are empirically uncorrelated with operationa...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, vector, alignment, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: CraniMem: Cranial Inspired Gated and Bounded Memory for Agentic Systems
item.description: arXiv:2603.15642v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language model (LLM) agents are increasingly deployed in long running workflows, where they must preserve user and task state across many turns. Many existing agent memory systems behave like external databases with ad hoc read/write rules, whic...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> company, model, memory, large language model, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: GSI Agent: Domain Knowledge Enhancement for Large Language Models in Green Stormwater Infrastructure
item.description: arXiv:2603.15643v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Green Stormwater Infrastructure (GSI) systems, such as permeable pavement, rain gardens, and bioretention facilities, require continuous inspection and maintenance to ensure long-term performance. However, domain knowledge about GSI is often scattered...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> fine-tuning, instruction, experiment, reasoning, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Did You Check the Right Pocket? Cost-Sensitive Store Routing for Memory-Augmented Agents
item.description: arXiv:2603.15658v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Memory-augmented agents maintain multiple specialized stores, yet most systems retrieve from all stores for every query, increasing cost and introducing irrelevant context. We formulate memory retrieval as a store-routing problem and evaluate it using...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> retrieval, memory, augmented, RAG, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: QV May Be Enough: Toward the Essence of Attention in LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2603.15665v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Starting from first principles and a linguistic perspective centered on part-of-speech (POS) and syntactic analysis, this paper explores and derives the underlying essence of the Query-Key-Value (QKV) mechanism within the Transformer architecture. Bas...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> transformer, experiment, model, attention, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: XLinear: Frequency-Enhanced MLP with CrossFilter for Robust Long-Range Forecasting
item.description: arXiv:2603.15645v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Time series forecasters are widely used across various domains. Among them, MLP (multi-layer perceptron)-based forecasters have been proven to be more robust to noise compared to Transformer-based forecasters. However, MLP struggles to capture complex...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> transformer, experiment, model, attention, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Alternating Reinforcement Learning with Contextual Rubric Rewards
item.description: arXiv:2603.15646v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reinforcement Learning with Rubric Rewards (RLRR) is a framework that extends conventional reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) and verifiable rewards (RLVR) by replacing scalar preference signals with structured, multi-dimensional, conte...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, model, RLHF, framework, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Steering Frozen LLMs: Adaptive Social Alignment via Online Prompt Routing
item.description: arXiv:2603.15647v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are typically governed by post-training alignment (e.g., RLHF or DPO), which yields a largely static policy during deployment and inference. However, real-world safety is a full-lifecycle problem: static defenses degrade a...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, model, analysis, prompt, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A federated learning framework with knowledge graph and temporal transformer for early sepsis prediction in multi-center ICUs
item.description: arXiv:2603.15651v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The early prediction of sepsis in intensive care unit (ICU) patients is crucial for improving survival rates. However, the development of accurate predictive models is hampered by data fragmentation across healthcare institutions and the complex, temp...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> transformer, API, model, RAG, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Discovering the Hidden Role of Gini Index In Prompt-based Classification
item.description: arXiv:2603.15654v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In classification tasks, the long-tailed minority classes usually offer the predictions that are most important. Yet these classes consistently exhibit low accuracies, whereas a few high-performing classes dominate the game. We pursue a foundational u...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> vision, zero-shot, few-shot, experiment, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Beyond Reward Suppression: Reshaping Steganographic Communication Protocols in MARL via Dynamic Representational Circuit Breaking
item.description: arXiv:2603.15655v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In decentralized Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL), steganographic collusion -- where agents develop private protocols to evade monitoring -- presents a critical AI safety threat. Existing defenses, limited to behavioral or reward layers, fail...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> API, experiment, analysis, framework, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Spectral Edge Dynamics of Training Trajectories: Signal--Noise Geometry Across Scales
item.description: arXiv:2603.15678v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Despite hundreds of millions of parameters, transformer training trajectories evolve within only a few coherent directions. We introduce \emph{Spectral Edge Dynamics} (SED) to measure this structure: rolling-window SVD of parameter updates reveals a s...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> transformer, model, GPT, framework, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Context-Engineering - "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.
item.description: "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt, prompt engineering, context window, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: ThinkSound - [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.
item.description: [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> audio, reasoning, CoT, framework, chain-of-thought | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: LightRAG - [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"
item.description: [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, augmented, retrieval | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: KAG - KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.
item.description: KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, reasoning, retrieval, knowledge base, vector | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Kiln - Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.
item.description: Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, fine-tuning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: R2R - SoTA production-ready AI retrieval system. Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with a RESTful API.

item.title: openlit - Open source platform for AI Engineering: OpenTelemetry-native LLM Observability, GPU Monitoring, Guardrails, Evaluations, Prompt Management, Vault, Playground. 🚀💻 Integrates with 50+ LLM Providers, VectorDBs, Agent Frameworks and GPUs.
item.description: Open source platform for AI Engineering: OpenTelemetry-native LLM Observability, GPU Monitoring, Guardrails, Evaluations, Prompt Management, Vault, Playground. 🚀💻 Integrates with 50+ LLM Providers, VectorDBs, Agent Frameworks and GPUs.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt, platform, vector, framework, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: How to Achieve Prototypical Birth and Death for OOD Detection?
item.description: arXiv:2603.15650v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Out-of-Distribution (OOD) detection is crucial for the secure deployment of machine learning models, and prototype-based learning methods are among the mainstream strategies for achieving OOD detection. Existing prototype-based learning methods genera...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, experiment, embedding | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Attribution-Guided Model Rectification of Unreliable Neural Network Behaviors
item.description: arXiv:2603.15656v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The performance of neural network models deteriorates due to their unreliable behavior on non-robust features of corrupted samples. Owing to their opaque nature, rectifying models to address this problem often necessitates arduous data cleaning and mo...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, experiment, RAG, framework, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: cosmos-reason1 - Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.

item.title: CTG-DB: An Ontology-Based Transformation of ClinicalTrials.gov to Enable Cross-Trial Drug Safety Analyses
item.description: arXiv:2603.15936v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: ClinicalTrials.gov (CT.gov) is the largest publicly accessible registry of clinical studies, yet its registry-oriented architecture and heterogeneous adverse event (AE) terminology limit systematic pharmacovigilance (PV) analytics. AEs are typically r...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> retrieval, framework, ICL, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: AIDABench: AI Data Analytics Benchmark
item.description: arXiv:2603.15636v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As AI-driven document understanding and processing tools become increasingly prevalent in real-world applications, the need for rigorous evaluation standards has grown increasingly urgent. Existing benchmarks and evaluations often focus on isolated ca...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, analysis, research, tool, ICL | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Form Follows Function: Recursive Stem Model
item.description: arXiv:2603.15641v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recursive reasoning models such as Hierarchical Reasoning Model (HRM) and Tiny Recursive Model (TRM) show that small, weight-shared networks can solve compute-heavy and NP puzzles by iteratively refining latent states, but their training typically rel...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> vision, model, reasoning, attention, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: DynaTrust: Defending Multi-Agent Systems Against Sleeper Agents via Dynamic Trust Graphs
item.description: arXiv:2603.15661v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Model-based Multi-Agent Systems (MAS) have demonstrated remarkable collaborative reasoning capabilities but introduce new attack surfaces, such as the sleeper agent, which behave benignly during routine operation and gradually accumulat...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, model, reasoning, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Tokenization Tradeoffs in Structured EHR Foundation Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.15644v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Foundation models for structured electronic health records (EHRs) are pretrained on longitudinal sequences of timestamped clinical events to learn adaptable patient representations. Tokenization -- how these timelines are converted into discrete model...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> transformer, model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: OpenViking - OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.
item.description: OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> memory, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Claude-Code-Everything-You-Need-to-Know - The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.
item.description: The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> example, step-by-step, prompt, tool, prompt engineering | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)
